notes: Oracle is a major enterprise software company. Products include Oracle Database, Java SE, WebLogic, MySQL, Oracle Cloud. Oracle releases security patches quarterly through Critical Patch Updates (CPUs). CPUs often fix hundreds of CVEs at once. WebLogic vulnerabilities are frequently exploited in the wild. Java vulnerabilities affect many applications beyond Oracle products. Oracle Linux is binary-compatible with RHEL.

data.description: Oracle's quarterly security patch releases bundling fixes for many CVEs across all Oracle products.
data.notes: Oracle releases CPUs quarterly on the third Tuesday of January, April, July, and October. Each CPU bundles fixes for many CVEs across all Oracle products. CPUs often fix hundreds of CVEs at once. WebLogic vulnerabilities are frequently exploited in the wild. For individual CVE alerts outside the quarterly schedule, see secid:advisory/oracle.com/alert.

data.description: Out-of-band security alerts for critical vulnerabilities requiring immediate attention, issued outside the regular CPU schedule.
data.notes: Security Alerts are issued outside the regular CPU schedule for critical vulnerabilities requiring immediate attention. Used for issues too urgent to wait for the quarterly release. Alert URLs use a lowercased form of the CVE ID in the path (e.g., CVE-2021-44228 becomes alert-cve-2021-44228).

children.description: CVE identifier for Oracle Security Alert

data.note: URL uses lowercased ID: CVE-2021-44228 becomes alert-cve-2021-44228 in the URL path

examples.note: Log4Shell -- Oracle's out-of-band security alert. URL lowercases the CVE prefix.

data.description: Security advisories for Oracle Linux, which is binary-compatible with Red Hat Enterprise Linux.
data.notes: Oracle Linux is binary-compatible with RHEL. Security fixes often mirror RHEL advisories. Supports Oracle Linux 9 (current), 8 (current), 7 (extended support). Ksplice provides rebootless kernel patching. Published same day as CPUs for relevant issues.

data.description: Security advisories covering Oracle VM Server for x86, including virtualization-specific and hypervisor security vulnerabilities.
data.notes: Oracle VM Server security advisories covering virtualization-specific vulnerabilities. Published with quarterly CPUs. No direct per-advisory lookup URL available -- advisories are listed on the Oracle security alerts page.

data.note: No direct lookup URL available. OVMSA advisories are listed on the Oracle security alerts page.
